findings.title: Potential panic from unwrap on stream lookup in SDK export
findings.issue: Uses .unwrap() on input_ctx.stream(video_stream_idx) and input_ctx.stream(audio_idx). If stream indices become invalid after seek operations or format changes, this would panic instead of gracefully handling the error.
findings.impact: Application crash during clip export if stream lookup fails after seek, leading to user data loss and poor user experience.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace .unwrap() with .with_context() to provide meaningful error messages and allow graceful error handling: input_ctx.stream(video_stream_idx).with_context(|| format!("Missing video stream {} after seek", video_stream_idx))?

findings.title: Potential panic from unwrap chain in scaler validation
findings.issue: Three consecutive .unwrap() calls on scaler.as_ref().unwrap() in condition checks. If scaler becomes None unexpectedly (e.g., after initialization failure), this would panic.
findings.impact: Application crash during thumbnail generation when attempting to validate scaler state.
findings.suggestedFix: Use if-let pattern or early return with error: if let Some(s) = scaler.as_ref() { s.input().format != sw.format() ... } else { return Err(anyhow!("Scaler not initialized")); }

findings.title: Potential panic from expect on scaler after lazy init
findings.issue: scaler.as_mut().expect("scaler") could panic if the previous scaler initialization silently failed and scaler remains None. The expect provides no recovery path.
findings.impact: Application crash during preview frame extraction when scaler is unexpectedly None.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace expect with proper error handling: scaler.as_mut().context("Scaler not initialized for preview")?.run(sw, &mut rgba_frame)?

findings.title: Unsafe FFmpeg raw packet manipulation needs explicit safety documentation
findings.issue: The unsafe block directly manipulates AVPacket fields including raw pointers. While the code appears correct (zeroing packet, setting data pointer from Bytes), there's no explicit safety comment documenting why this is safe.
findings.impact: Memory safety issues if assumptions about packet lifetime or Bytes internal representation change. Could lead to use-after-free or memory corruption.
findings.suggestedFix: Add explicit SAFETY comment: '// SAFETY: We zero the AVPacket before use. packet.data comes from Bytes::as_ptr() which remains valid for the duration of this function since the EncodedPacket holds a Bytes reference. The raw_packet is consumed by av_interleaved_write_frame which copies data internally.'

findings.title: Unsafe codec_tag clearing needs explicit safety documentation
findings.location: Lines 95, 113 - stream mapping for stream copy
findings.issue: unsafe { (*out_stream.parameters().as_mut_ptr()).codec_tag = 0; } modifies FFmpeg codec parameters directly. The comment mentions this is a 'standard pattern' but doesn't document safety invariants.
findings.impact: Potential undefined behavior if FFmpeg's AVCodecParameters structure layout changes or if the pointer becomes invalid during modification.
findings.suggestedFix: Add explicit SAFETY comment: '// SAFETY: We hold exclusive access to out_stream via add_stream(). FFmpeg documentation indicates codec_tag may be cleared for stream copy. The pointer remains valid because we own the output context. Parameters are immediately overwritten by set_parameters() with valid input codec params.'

findings.title: Silent ignoring of EOF errors in audio encoder flush
findings.issue: encoder.send_eof().ok() silently ignores any error from sending EOF to the encoder. While EOF errors are often benign, completely ignoring them could mask issues like encoder state corruption.
findings.impact: Potential incomplete audio encoding or missing final frames if EOF signal fails, with no diagnostic logging.
findings.suggestedFix: Log any EOF errors for diagnostics: if let Err(e) = encoder.send_eof() { tracing::warn!("Encoder EOF signal failed: {}", e); } or use .ok() with a comment explaining why EOF errors are intentionally ignored.

findings.title: Move-or-copy lacks atomicity guarantee for final output placement
findings.issue: The function first removes destination, then tries rename, then falls back to copy+remove. If rename fails and copy succeeds but remove fails, we have duplicate files. No transactional semantics.
findings.impact: Potential leftover temporary files consuming disk space, or partial output file if operations fail mid-way.
findings.suggestedFix: Consider using a temp file adjacent to destination with atomic rename on Windows, or add explicit cleanup tracking. At minimum, log any cleanup failures for manual intervention.

findings.title: Encoder recreated per range in SDK export
findings.issue: For each keep_range, decoders and scalers are recreated from scratch after seek. This adds overhead for each range boundary.
findings.impact: Increased export time for clips with many trimmed segments (e.g., removing multiple sections).
findings.suggestedFix: Consider caching decoder/scaler setup and only recreating when format changes, or document that the overhead is acceptable for correctness (seek invalidates decoder state).

findings.title: Vec allocations in audio mixing without reuse
findings.issue: Allocates mixed_i32, mixed_i16, and pcm_buffer vectors per chunk. While there's shrink_to_fit for pcm_buffer, the other buffers don't reuse across chunks.
findings.impact: Memory churn during audio encoding for long clips, though chunking at 1-second bounds helps.
findings.suggestedFix: Consider reusing mixed_i32 and mixed_i16 buffers across chunks similar to pcm_buffer, or document that current approach is acceptable given chunk sizing.

findings.title: OutputError enum is overly simplistic
findings.issue: OutputError has only a single Msg(String) variant, losing error categorization. Using thiserror with #[source] would preserve error chains.
findings.impact: Error diagnostics lose information about the original error source, making debugging harder.
findings.suggestedFix: Expand OutputError to include variants for common error types: Io(std::io::Error), Ffmpeg(ffmpeg::Error), Muxing(String), etc., with #[source] attributes for error chaining.

findings.title: Missing error handling for thumbnail save failure
findings.issue: Thumbnail generation failure is only logged as a warning and doesn't affect the main save result. While this is acceptable behavior, there's no option for users to retry thumbnail generation.
findings.impact: Clips may appear without thumbnails in gallery, but users have no recourse.
findings.suggestedFix: Document this behavior clearly, or add a 'refresh thumbnail' option in gallery for cases where thumbnail generation failed.

findings.title: Silent ignoring of fragmented MP4 cleanup failure
findings.issue: let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&fragmented_path) inside if let Err(err) silently ignores any filesystem errors. While cleanup failure isn't critical, users may accumulate orphaned files.
findings.impact: Potential accumulation of orphaned .fragmented.mp4 files in save directory over time.
findings.suggestedFix: Log cleanup failures at debug level or track cleanup failures for periodic maintenance: if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(&fragmented_path) { debug!("Failed to clean up fragmented MP4: {}", e); }

findings.title: Hardcoded retry constants without configuration
findings.issue: Retry limits (8 attempts, 125ms sleep) are hardcoded constants. Users with slow systems may need more retries, but cannot configure this.
findings.impact: May fail to save clips on systems with high encoder lag, with no user recourse.
findings.suggestedFix: Consider adding advanced configuration for retry behavior, or at least document why these values were chosen (balance between wait time and success rate).

findings.title: Magic number for audio packet jitter tolerance
findings.issue: AUDIO_PACKET_JITTER_TOLERANCE_FRAMES = 8 frames is a magic number without documented rationale. Why 8 specifically?
findings.impact: Hard to understand or tune audio synchronization behavior without documentation.
findings.suggestedFix: Add comment documenting rationale: '// 8 frames at 48kHz = ~0.17ms. This tolerance accommodates WASAPI buffer latency variations without snapping packets to wrong positions.'

findings.title: Good use of WorkDirGuard for cleanup on failure
findings.issue: POSITIVE: Implements RAII pattern for export work directory cleanup. The Drop implementation ensures temp files are removed even if export fails or panics.
findings.impact: Prevents accumulation of temporary export files in system temp directory.
findings.suggestedFix: No fix needed - this is a good pattern that should be retained.

findings.title: Good memory management patterns in clip saver
findings.location: Lines 45-50, throughout - aggressively_drop_packets and explicit drops
findings.issue: POSITIVE: Implements aggressive memory cleanup during clip saving. Explicit drops of TrackedSnapshot release memory early, preventing OOM during large clip saves.
findings.impact: Prevents memory exhaustion during clip save operations.
findings.suggestedFix: No fix needed - this is a well-designed pattern for the replay buffer architecture.

findings.title: Good error context usage throughout output module
findings.issue: POSITIVE: Extensive use of .with_context() for meaningful error messages. Errors include file paths and operation descriptions, aiding debugging.
findings.impact: Clear error diagnostics when file I/O or encoding operations fail.
findings.suggestedFix: No fix needed - continue this pattern for new code.

findings.title: Bytes-based packet handling for zero-copy muxing
findings.issue: POSITIVE: Using bytes::Bytes for packet data allows cheap clones during muxing. This prevents deep copies of large video frames during save operations.
findings.impact: Efficient memory usage during clip save; O(1) clone operations.
findings.suggestedFix: No fix needed - this matches the buffer module's architecture.

findings.title: Well-structured separation between SDK export and stream copy
findings.issue: POSITIVE: Clear separation between stream copy (no re-encoding) and full export (with encoding). This allows fast export when quality preservation is desired.
findings.impact: Users can choose fast export (stream copy) or quality-controlled export.
findings.suggestedFix: No fix needed - good architectural separation.

findings.title: Comprehensive test coverage for audio mixing
findings.issue: POSITIVE: Extensive unit tests for audio packet mixing including jitter tolerance, gap preservation, and truncation edge cases.
findings.impact: Audio synchronization behavior is well-tested and documented through tests.
findings.suggestedFix: No fix needed - good test coverage for critical audio path.

keyTakeaways: The output module has solid architecture with good error handling patterns (.with_context), memory management (explicit drops, Bytes), and cleanup (WorkDirGuard RAII).
Three High-severity issues stem from unwrap/expect usage in SDK export paths that could cause panics under edge cases (stream lookup failures, scaler validation).
Medium-severity issues include missing safety documentation for unsafe blocks, and non-atomic file operations.
The module correctly implements the critical clip saving pipeline with proper retry logic, keyframe validation, and memory cleanup.
Audio handling has comprehensive tests and handles jitter/gaps correctly.
